mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns exceed 55% of triggered instances, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains positive despite the threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand, producing negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive forward returns in over 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during post-shock recovery phases reflect rapid capacity utilization and earnings growth, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of recovery-phase observations, or momentum decays within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 205 signal entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in over 65% of high-inflation threshold breaches, invalidating the valuation compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total employment and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market deterioration, forecasting negative quarterly equity returns due to earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52% over rolling 5-year windows, or earnings revisions remain positive during divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of GDP expansion quarters, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices falling below 110 indicate severe manufacturing contraction that triggers supply chain disruptions and earnings warnings, driving negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 60% of threshold breach instances, or the signal shows no incremental predictive value over a simple moving average.
